drafts.draft: The smallest businesses get the worst tools. Enterprise gets a Salesforce admin team. A solo electrician gets a notebook and a missed-call list.
What SMBs want isn't an AI agent. They want a thing that picks up the phone, books the job, and stops bothering them.
Speed of reply beats quality of reply. A two-line answer in two minutes converts better than a perfect answer at lunch tomorrow.

drafts.target_text: Call me crazy but I think nerfing AI models should be illegal. Imagine buying a car and the manufacturer remotely downgrading your engine 6 months later.
drafts.draft: Counter: you didn't buy the model, you rented inference. The car analogy only works if you also bought a battery, a charger, and a private dealership. The lease framing is more honest.

drafts.target_text: How to use Google's NEW open source Design.md + AI Skills to make your startup look like a 100 million company in 1 hour. Design.md captures the soul of a brand.
drafts.draft: The real win isn't looking like a 100M company. It's that your second hire ships on-brand without three weeks of design reviews. Brand consistency is a hiring multiplier.

drafts.target_text: Which one would you prioritize first? Users or Revenue?
drafts.draft: Revenue. One paying customer tells you more than 1000 free users. Free users are a vanity metric pretending to be product feedback.

drafts.target_text: It's a charming document, too. Purpose of account use: personal use, business use, nuclear weapons development, international wire transfers, chemical weapons development.
drafts.draft: The wild thing is that checkbox isn't there to catch the actual proliferator. It's there so when the FBI shows up six years later, the bank can say we asked.

drafts.target_text: Announcing the Lenny's Newsletter x Replit Buildathon. Build something awesome with my podcast and newsletter data using Replit, and win fabulous prizes.
drafts.draft: Curious if you'll let people fine-tune on the corpus or just RAG-query it. The first option is a much more interesting Buildathon, the second is mostly a UI exercise.

drafts.target_text: I do genuinely love this stuff. How can you not.
drafts.draft: The fintech-adjacent compliance content is your best work and I will die on this hill.

drafts.target_text: Still waiting at what point these companies will just ask to do a credit card auth. At this rate it would be faster to get it done for services I WANT to use.
drafts.draft: Stripe Identity does this in about 90 seconds. The reason platforms haven't switched isn't tech, it's that the captcha is also a soft signup-friction tool to keep their funnel numbers looking better.
drafts.reason: GergelyOrosz / credit card auth replacing captcha

drafts.target_text: As a founder, which one takes most of your energy? Building, selling, marketing, or thinking?
drafts.draft: Selling. Building is satisfying so it feels free. Selling is uncomfortable so it costs energy. The thing that costs energy is usually the thing that pays the bills.

drafts.target_text: Watching jarredsumner and bcherny at Code w/ Code talking about robobun, the Bun project's GitHub bot that's now made more contributions to Bun than Jarred has.
drafts.draft: The interesting metric isn't contributions count, it's whether reviewers spend less time on robobun PRs than on human ones. If yes, the bot has actually crossed a threshold.

drafts.target_text: Our AI VP of Marketing is better than me now. He now does some light marketing campaigns autonomously. And 10K gives us 3 pretty darn good ideas a day.
drafts.draft: What's the human-in-the-loop ratio? 3 ideas a day is one number. The interesting one is how many of those 3 ship without you touching them.

drafts.target_text: Planning the path to 10+ Billion with jeiting elwatto + Team RevenueCat
drafts.draft: RevenueCat at 10B would be the most boring success story in SaaS, which is exactly why it'll happen. The unsexy infrastructure plays compound the longest.

drafts.target_text: I don't think they'll be taking Q&A so I'll ask here: jarredsumner do you end up actually reading all of that chatter between the review bots on a PR?
drafts.draft: Adjacent question for jarred: at what review-bot density does the chatter become noise that hides the actual disagreement? There's a sweet spot somewhere.
